mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of Kubernetes clusters on AWS. It is ideal for users who want to leverage AWS's infrastructure and services while maintaining control over their Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services, including EKS.

```bash

curl "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/AWSCLIV2.pkg" -o "AWSCLIV2.pkg"

sudo installer -pkg AWSCLIV2.pkg -target /

aws --version

```

> Download the AWS CLI package.
> Install the AWS CLI.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-ClusterRole --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace ACCOUNT_ID with your AWS account ID and subnet IDs with your VPC subnets.

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the new EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks if the nodes in the EKS cluster are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/eks-charts/master/stable/metrics-server/metrics-server.yaml

```

> This applies the cluster autoscaler configuration.
> This installs the metrics server for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

aws ec2 delete-security-group --group-id sg-12345678

```

> This command deletes the EKS cluster.
> Delete any orphaned security groups.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the Kubernetes version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: No resources found in kube-system namespace
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the metrics server is not installed.

**Fix:**
Install the metrics server using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/eks-charts/master/stable/metrics-server/metrics-server.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status using 'aws eks describe-cluster --name my-cluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Insufficient CPU resources
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or adjust resource requests for your pods.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved IAM roles for service accounts.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.28 or 1.29, ensure that your workloads are compatible with 1.31.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the AWS CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
